name: Mapbox
description: Drive Mapbox APIs (geocoding, directions, isochrones, matrix, tilequery) from any AI agent. 8 tools, access-token query auth.
instructions:
This connector uses Mapbox's various REST APIs (docs.mapbox.com/api/).

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to https://account.mapbox.com → **Access tokens → Create a token**.
2. Pick scopes: usually `styles:read` + `fonts:read` + `geocoding` + `directions` + `matrix` + `isochrone` + `tilequery`.
3. Copy the token (starts with `pk.`). Set `MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN`.

**Authentication**: query-string `?access_token=...`.

**Coordinate convention** (Mapbox-specific): **longitude FIRST**, then latitude. The order is `lng,lat`, NOT `lat,lng` like Google Maps. Trip up on this and your results land in the wrong country.

**Geocoding v6 (forward + reverse)** is now the default — uses `/search/geocode/v6/forward` and `/reverse`. v5 still works at `/geocoding/v5/mapbox.places/...` but is in maintenance.

**Directions profiles**: driving, walking, cycling, driving-traffic (live traffic, premium tier).

**Cost**: all Mapbox APIs have a generous free tier (50k geocoding/month, 100k directions/month). Beyond that, paid per request — check pricing page. Each API call counts as 1 'API hit'.

**Out of scope here**: tile rendering (use Mapbox GL JS), uploads, custom datasets, navigation SDK.

tools.name: mapbox_geocode_forward
tools.description: Forward geocoding (address text → coordinates). Returns ranked candidates with [longitude, latitude], place_name, address components.

country.description: Comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes to limit results (e.g. 'us,ca').

proximity.description: 'lng,lat' coordinate to bias results toward (e.g. user's current location).

language.description: IETF tag for the language of results (en, de, fr, es, ja, zh, etc.).

tools.name: mapbox_geocode_reverse
tools.description: Reverse geocoding (coordinates → address). Pass longitude + latitude.

tools.name: mapbox_directions
tools.description: Compute driving / walking / cycling directions between waypoints. Returns route geometry, duration (seconds), distance (meters), turn-by-turn steps.

tools.name: mapbox_isochrone
tools.description: Get the area reachable within N minutes from a starting point (an 'isochrone' — like 'how far can I drive in 15 min?'). Returns a GeoJSON polygon.

contours_minutes.description: Comma-separated minute thresholds (e.g. '5,10,15'). Max 4 contours, max 60 min each.

polygons.description: If true return polygons (default), if false return linestrings.

tools.name: mapbox_matrix
tools.description: Compute travel-time and distance MATRIX between many origins and destinations (e.g. 5 warehouses × 100 customers = 500 cells). Returns durations[][] in seconds and distances[][] in meters.

sources.description: Comma-separated 0-based indices into coordinates to use as sources. 'all' to use all.

tools.name: mapbox_static_image
tools.description: Generate a static PNG/JPG map of a location with optional pin overlays. Returns the image binary URL.

username.description: Mapbox style username (typically 'mapbox' for built-in styles).

tools.name: mapbox_tilequery
tools.description: Query features in a vector tileset at a specific point — e.g. find which neighborhood a coordinate falls in.

tools.name: mapbox_list_styles
tools.description: List custom map styles owned by your account. Use to discover style IDs for the static-image endpoint.
